sections.heading: Definition of Consumer
sections.body_text: A consumer is defined as any person who buys goods or avails services for consideration, including offline and online transactions, but does not include a person who obtains goods for resale or commercial purposes.

sections.heading: Definition of Deficiency
sections.body_text: Deficiency means any fault, imperfection, shortcoming, or inadequacy in the quality, nature, or manner of performance of a service that is required to be maintained under any law or contract.

sections.heading: Definition of Defect
sections.body_text: Defect means any fault, imperfection, or shortcoming in the quality, quantity, potency, purity, or standard of goods, which is required to be maintained under any law or contract.

sections.heading: Definition of Unfair Trade Practice
sections.body_text: Unfair trade practice includes any deceptive or unfair method or practice such as false representation, misleading advertisements, or false warranties, which mislead consumers.

sections.heading: Definition of Restrictive Trade Practice
sections.body_text: Restrictive trade practice refers to any trade practice that manipulates the price or supply of goods or services, or imposes unjustified costs or restrictions on consumers.

sections.heading: Consumer Rights
sections.body_text: Consumer rights include the right to safety, information, choice, redressal, consumer education, and the right to be heard in appropriate forums.

sections.heading: Jurisdiction of District Commission
sections.body_text: The District Commission has jurisdiction to entertain complaints where the value of goods or services paid as consideration does not exceed one crore rupees.

sections.heading: Jurisdiction of State Commission
sections.body_text: The State Commission has jurisdiction to entertain complaints where the value of goods or services paid as consideration exceeds one crore rupees but does not exceed ten crore rupees.

sections.heading: Jurisdiction of National Commission
sections.body_text: The National Commission has jurisdiction to entertain complaints where the value of goods or services paid as consideration exceeds ten crore rupees.

sections.heading: Appeals to State Commission
sections.body_text: Any person aggrieved by an order of the District Commission may prefer an appeal to the State Commission within 45 days from the date of the order.

sections.heading: Appeals to National Commission
sections.body_text: Any person aggrieved by an order of the State Commission may prefer an appeal to the National Commission within 30 days from the date of the order.

sections.heading: Appeals to Supreme Court
sections.body_text: Any person aggrieved by an order of the National Commission may prefer an appeal to the Supreme Court within 30 days from the date of the order.

sections.heading: Establishment of Mediation Cells
sections.body_text: Mediation cells shall be established in each District, State, and National Commission to facilitate the resolution of disputes through mediation.

sections.heading: Reference to Mediation
sections.body_text: A consumer dispute may be referred to mediation if both parties consent, and the mediation process shall be completed within 30 days.

sections.heading: Product Liability
sections.body_text: A product manufacturer, service provider, or seller shall be liable to compensate for harm caused to a consumer due to defective goods or deficient services.

sections.heading: Liability of Product Manufacturer
sections.body_text: A product manufacturer shall be liable if the product has a manufacturing defect, is defective in design, or does not conform to express warranties.

sections.heading: Liability of Product Service Provider
sections.body_text: A product service provider shall be liable if the service provided is deficient, does not meet safety standards, or causes harm to the consumer.

sections.heading: Liability of Product Seller
sections.body_text: A product seller shall be liable if the seller has exercised substantial control over the product, altered it, or failed to provide adequate instructions or warnings.

sections.heading: Penalties for Non-Compliance
sections.body_text: Any person who fails to comply with the orders of the District, State, or National Commission shall be punishable with imprisonment up to three years or a fine up to one lakh rupees, or both.

sections.heading: Measures to Prevent Unfair Trade Practices in E-Commerce
sections.body_text: The Central Government may take measures to prevent unfair trade practices in e-commerce, including ensuring transparency and consumer protection.

sections.heading: Limitation Period for Filing Complaints
sections.body_text: A complaint shall be filed within two years from the date on which the cause of action arises, subject to the discretion of the Commission to condone delays.

sections.heading: Penalty for False or Misleading Advertisement
sections.body_text: Any manufacturer or service provider who publishes a false or misleading advertisement shall be liable to a penalty of up to ten lakh rupees and imprisonment up to two years.

sections.heading: Cognizance of Offences
sections.body_text: No court shall take cognizance of an offence under this Act except on a complaint made by the Central Authority or any officer authorized by it.

sections.heading: Power to Make Rules
sections.body_text: The Central Government may, by notification, make rules for carrying out the provisions of this Act.
